goal: Comprehensive E2E Testing of gru-ai v0.2.0

risks: Agent benchmark dimension is unbounded — 'build a new web app' + 'improve existing code' + multiple agent configs + multiple platforms creates a combinatorial explosion.
Showing 'actual products' (web apps agents built) requires serving/screenshotting output from multiple test runs.
Multi-platform init depends on those tools being installable and functional in our test env.
over_engineering_flags: CEO wants comprehensive agent benchmarks with multiple dimensions x configs x platforms. Keep it as parameterized bash scripts, not a custom benchmarking engine.
Results reporting: markdown summary table with timing, pass/fail, and links to output dirs is sufficient for v1.
challenges.recommendation: Simplify — bash scripts with markdown output. No test frameworks, no CI integration, no automated browser testing.

projects.title: Fix Known Bugs Before Testing
projects.scope_summary: Fix 3 known bugs: (1) server/config.ts ignoring PORT env var, (2) gruai.config.json missing preset/platform fields, (3) agent-registry.json CEO entry causing N+1 agent count.

projects.title: E2E Orchestrator + Deterministic Test Dimensions (1-2, 4, 6-7)
projects.scope_summary: Build run-e2e.sh orchestrator with --dim flag. Implement deterministic test dimensions: fresh install/init (3 presets), server startup/dashboard (HTTP, WS, assets), directive JSON schema validation, multi-platform init (4 variants), benchmark timing harness.

projects.title: Agent Benchmark Suite — Bug Fix, Build, Improve
projects.scope_summary: Comprehensive agent benchmark: (1) bug-fix using existing fixture, (2) build-new-app from spec, (3) improve-existing with refactoring directive. Each runs across agent configs (starter/standard/full) and platforms. Output: actual artifacts in results dirs for CEO inspection, markdown summary with timing/pass rates/quality.

projects.title: Chrome MCP UI Verification Checklist for CEO
projects.scope_summary: Structured manual verification checklist for CEO to walk through in Chrome MCP. Covers dashboard, agent cards, directive status, WebSocket updates, responsive layout. Documentation only — CEO executes.
